sourceMovie.title: The Dry
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Dry (2021) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Robert Connolly's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Big Sleep
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Dry clicked for you, The Big Sleep (1946) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Dry?
faq.answer: Try The Big Sleep, Crooked House, American Psycho—each shares DNA with The Dry while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Dry?
faq.answer: The Dry (2021) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Dry part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Dry Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
